skills.primary_deduction: Pike in pre-flight (0.15), arm bend on table (0.10), arch in repulsion (0.10), slight leg separation in repulsion (0.05), arch in post-flight (0.10), slight leg separation in post-flight (0.05), chest down on landing (0.10), knee bend on landing (0.10), small hop forward on landing (0.10).

coaching_summary: The gymnast completed the Front Handspring Vault without a fall. However, significant form breaks were observed throughout the vault, starting with a pike in the pre-flight phase and an arch maintained through the post-flight. Arms were not fully straight on the table. The landing showed multiple deductions including a low chest, bent knees, and a hop forward. Focus on tightening the body shape and improving landing control.
top_3_fixes: Maintain a tight, hollow body shape from board contact through post-flight to eliminate pike and arch.
Ensure arms are fully extended and strong during table contact and repulsion.
Focus on a controlled, stuck landing with chest up and knees locked.
